exercises.title: Nested Loop Iteration Count
exercises.description: For each iteration of the outer loop, the inner loop runs completely. Trace the iterations by printing i and j.
hints: For each value of i, j goes through 1, 2, 3 completely.

exercises.title: Multiplication Table (5×5)
exercises.description: Use nested loops to print a 5×5 multiplication table. Each row should be on one line.
hints: print(f"{value:4d}", end="") prints the value right-aligned in 4 characters without a newline.
print() at the end of the inner loop creates the newline for each row.

exercises.title: Grading All Students
exercises.description: Use nested loops to process a grid of student grades and print each student's average.
exercises.starterCode:
students = ["Alice", "Bob", "Carol"]
grades = [
    [85, 92, 78],   # Alice's grades
    [70, 88, 95],   # Bob's grades
    [91, 83, 87],   # Carol's grades
]

for i in range(len(students)):
    total = 0
    for grade in grades[i]:
        total += grade
    average = total / len(grades[i])
    print(f"{students[i]}: {average:.1f}")
hints: The outer loop iterates over students by index.
The inner loop sums the grades for that student.
Divide by len(grades[i]) to get the average.

warmup.title: Quick Recall: Inner Loop Runs Completely for Each Outer Iteration
warmup.description: The inner loop resets and runs from the beginning for each outer loop iteration.
